title: Getting Started
text: need Miniconda. Download installer OS link follow -screen steps.

title: Getting Started
text: Open Terminal run:
code: git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git cd volaris brew install gsl libomp conda create -n volaris python=3.11 conda activate volaris conda install -c conda-forge numpy cython cd python pip install -e .

title: Getting Started
text: Open Terminal run:
code: git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git cd volaris sudo apt-get install libgsl-dev conda create -n volaris python=3.11 conda activate volaris conda install -c conda-forge numpy cython cd python pip install -e .

title: Getting Started
text: Open Anaconda Prompt (installed Miniconda) run:
code: git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git cd volaris conda create -n volaris python=3.11 conda activate volaris conda install -c conda-forge gsl numpy cython cd python pip install -e .

title: Getting Started
text: Open Terminal run: R: Terminal:
code: git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git cd volaris brew install r gsl install.packages("Rcpp") R CMD INSTALL R/Volaris

title: Getting Started
text: Open Terminal run: R: Terminal:
code: git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git cd volaris sudo apt-get install r-base libgsl-dev install.packages("Rcpp") R CMD INSTALL R/Volaris

title: Getting Started
text: Install Rtools: choose version matching R version (e.g. Rtools45 R 4.5.x). Download run installer. Open Rtools Bash: press Start, search Rtools, open Rtools Bash (terminal window). Rtools Bash, install GSL: Clone repository (still Rtools Bash): Open R install required R package: Back PowerShell (regular Windows terminal), repo root:
code: pacman -S mingw-w64-ucrt-x86_64-gsl git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git cd volaris install.packages("Rcpp") R CMD INSTALL R\Volaris

title: Authors and Citation
text: Barbara Gawlik. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Gawlik B (2026). Volaris: High-Performance Library Quantitative Finance. R package version 1.0.0, https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {Volaris: High-Performance Library for Quantitative Finance},   author = {Barbara Gawlik},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/}, }

title: High-Performance Library for Quantitative Finance
text: Volaris high-performance library quantitative finance – option pricing, volatility estimation, stochastic simulation, numerical methods, compiled C/C++ backend shared Python R. Whether pricing derivatives, fitting volatility models, running Monte Carlo simulations, Volaris gives C-speed without leaving Python R. want hands-introduction topics, check notebooks directory worked examples. Available : Python package – volaris (python/) R package – Volaris (R/Volaris/)

title: High-Performance Library for Quantitative Finance
text: Black-Scholes (Greeks calculation) Binomial Tree Monte Carlo (European, Asian, Barrier) Heston stochastic volatility Historical volatility (Close--Close, Parkinson, Garman-Klass, Yang-Zhang) Implied volatility GARCH(1,1) fit & forecast Geometric Brownian Motion (plain, antithetic) Merton jump-diffusion MCMC (Metropolis-Hastings) Root-finding (Newton-Raphson, Bisection) Numerical integration (Gauss quadrature, GSL adaptive)

title: High-Performance Library for Quantitative Finance
code: volaris/ │ ├── src/                            # Shared C/C++ source code │   ├── pricing/                    #   Black-Scholes, Binomial Tree, Heston, Monte Carlo │   ├── volatility/                 #   Historical vol, Implied vol, GARCH │   ├── simulation/                 #   GBM, Jump-Diffusion, MCMC │   ├── numerical/                  #   Root-finding, Integration │   └── utils/                      #   LCG random number generator │ ├── python/                         # Python package │   ├── volaris/ │   │   ├── _core.pyx               #   Cython wrapper (C modules) │   │   └── _heston_garch.pyx       #   Cython wrapper (C++ modules) │   ├── tests/ │   └── pyproject.toml │ ├── R/Volaris/                      # R package │   ├── R/                          #   R wrapper functions │   ├── src/                        #   C/C++ sources (copy from src/) + R wrappers │   │   ├── volaris_r.c             #     R/C API wrapper (C modules) │   │   └── heston_garch_rcpp.cpp   #     Rcpp wrapper (C++ modules) |   ├── man/                        #   Roxygen2-generated documentation |   ├── vignettes/                  #   Getting-started guide │   ├── tests/ |   ├── _pkgdown.yml                #   pkgdown site configuration │   └── DESCRIPTION │ ├── notebooks/                      # Jupyter notebooks with usage examples ├── .github/workflows/              # CI: R CMD check + pytest on Linux, macOS, Windows └── CHANGELOG.md

title: High-Performance Library for Quantitative Finance
text: C/C++ backend (required packages) GNU GSL >= 2.0: numerical integration OpenMP: parallelism Monte Carlo GARCH Python Python >= 3.9 NumPy >= 1.21 Cython R R >= 4.0.0 Rcpp Testing (optional) Python: pytest R: testthat

title: High-Performance Library for Quantitative Finance
text: Benchmarked Windows 11, Python 3.12, NumPy 2.x, R 4.5.0. Python R garch_fit faster, R 4.x compiles inner loop fly, making sequential GARCH recurrence nearly fast C. sequential dependency prevents vectorisation either implementation.

title: High-Performance Library for Quantitative Finance
text: See notebooks/ directory full worked examples: notebooks/01_black_scholes.ipynb – option pricing Black-Scholes Binomial Tree notebooks/02_implied_volatility_surface.ipynb – volatility surface construction notebooks/03_monte_carlo_pricing.ipynb – Monte Carlo pricing exotic options

title: High-Performance Library for Quantitative Finance
text: MIT, see LICENSE.

what: Volaris: High-performance library for quantitative finance — Volaris-package
title: Volaris: High-performance library for quantitative finance — Volaris-package
text: Fast derivatives pricing, volatility estimation, stochastic simulation, numerical computation compiled C/C++ backend. Fast derivatives pricing, volatility estimation, stochastic simulation, numerical computation compiled C/C++ backend.

title: Volaris: High-performance library for quantitative finance — Volaris-package
text: Maintainer: Barbara Gawlik basia.m.gawlik@gmail.com

title: Binomial tree options pricing — binomial_price
text: Computes theoretical price option using binomial tree model.

title: Binomial tree options pricing — binomial_price
code: binomial_price(S, K, T, r, q, sigma, N, is_call, is_american)

title: Binomial tree options pricing — binomial_price
text: S numeric scalar: current price underlying asset K numeric scalar: strike price option T numeric scalar: time expiration option (years) r numeric scalar: risk-free interest rate (annualized) q numeric scalar: continuous dividend yield underlying asset (annualized) sigma numeric scalar: volatility underlying asset (annualized) N integer scalar: number steps binomial tree is_call integer scalar: 1, price call option; 0, price put option is_american integer scalar: 1, price American option; 0, price European option

title: Binomial tree options pricing — binomial_price
text: Returns numeric scalar range (0, +inf) representing price option.

title: Black-Scholes delta — bs_delta
text: Computes delta option: first derivative option price respect underlying asset price (dV/dS).

title: Black-Scholes delta — bs_delta
code: bs_delta(S, K, T, r, sigma, is_call)

title: Black-Scholes delta — bs_delta
text: S numeric scalar: current price underlying asset K numeric scalar: strike price option T numeric scalar: time expiration option (years) r numeric scalar: risk-free interest rate (annualized) sigma numeric scalar: volatility underlying asset (annualized) is_call integer scalar: 1, compute delta call option; 0, compute delta put option

title: Black-Scholes delta — bs_delta
text: Returns numeric scalar range (-1, 1) representing delta option.

title: Black-Scholes gamma — bs_gamma
text: Computes gamma option: second derivative option price respect underlying asset price (d^2V/dS^2).

text: S numeric scalar: current price underlying asset K numeric scalar: strike price option T numeric scalar: time expiration option (years) r numeric scalar: risk-free interest rate (annualized) sigma numeric scalar: volatility underlying asset (annualized)

text: Returns numeric scalar range (0, +inf) representing gamma option.

title: GARCH(1,1) parameter calibration — garch_fit
text: Fits GARCH(1,1) model historical return series via maximum likelihood estimation using Nelder-Mead optimizer.

title: GARCH(1,1) parameter calibration — garch_fit
text: returns numeric vector: log-return series

title: GARCH(1,1) parameter calibration — garch_fit
text: Returns list elements: omega, alpha, beta, log_likelihood, converged, persistence.

title: GARCH(1,1) conditional variances forecasting — garch_forecast
text: Computes conditional variance forecasts fitted GARCH(1,1) parameters.

title: GARCH(1,1) conditional variances forecasting — garch_forecast
text: omega numeric scalar: omega parameter garch_fit() alpha numeric scalar: alpha parameter garch_fit() beta numeric scalar: beta parameter garch_fit() returns numeric vector: original return series h integer scalar: forecast horizon (steps ahead)

title: GARCH(1,1) conditional variances forecasting — garch_forecast
text: Returns numeric vector length h conditional variance forecasts.

title: GARCH(1,1) historical conditional variances calculation — garch_variances
GARCH(1,1) historical conditional variances calculation — garch_variances
text: omega numeric scalar: omega parameter garch_fit() alpha numeric scalar: alpha parameter garch_fit() beta numeric scalar: beta parameter garch_fit() returns numeric vector: original return series

title: GARCH(1,1) historical conditional variances calculation — garch_variances
text: Returns numeric vector length equal returns conditional variances.

what: Heston stochastic volatility options pricing — heston_price
title: Heston stochastic volatility options pricing — heston_price
text: Computes theoretical price European option using Heston stochastic volatility model via characteristic function integration.

title: Heston stochastic volatility options pricing — heston_price
code: heston_price(S, K, T, r, v0, kappa, theta, xi, rho, is_call)

title: Heston stochastic volatility options pricing — heston_price
text: S numeric scalar: current price underlying asset K numeric scalar: strike price option T numeric scalar: time expiration option (years) r numeric scalar: risk-free interest rate (annualized) v0 numeric scalar: initial variance kappa numeric scalar: mean reversion speed variance theta numeric scalar: long-run variance (mean reversion level) xi numeric scalar: volatility variance rho numeric scalar: correlation asset variance processes is_call integer scalar: 1, price call option; 0, price put option

title: Heston stochastic volatility options pricing — heston_price
text: Returns numeric scalar range (0, +inf) representing price option.

title: Heston stochastic volatility options pricing — heston_price
Implied volatility (Black-Scholes inversion) — implied_vol
text: Computes implied volatility option inverting Black-Scholes formula using Newton-Raphson.

title: Implied volatility (Black-Scholes inversion) — implied_vol
code: implied_vol(market_price, S, K, T, r, is_call)

title: Implied volatility (Black-Scholes inversion) — implied_vol
text: market_price numeric scalar: observed market price option S numeric scalar: current price underlying asset K numeric scalar: strike price option T numeric scalar: time expiration (years) r numeric scalar: risk-free interest rate (annualized) is_call integer scalar: 1 call, 0 put

title: Implied volatility (Black-Scholes inversion) — implied_vol
text: Returns numeric scalar: implied volatility (annualized).

title: Gauss quadrature integratinon — integrate_gauss
text: Integrates f [, b] using Gauss quadrature.

title: Gauss quadrature integratinon — integrate_gauss
text: f function: scalar -> scalar whose integral seek numeric: bracket left endpoint b numeric: bracket right endpoint n_points integer: number quadrature points (default 10)

title: Gauss quadrature integratinon — integrate_gauss
text: Returns numeric scalar representing approximate integral f [, b].

title: GSL QAGS adaptive integration — integrate_gsl
text: Integrates f [, b] using GSL QAGS adaptive quadrature.

title: GSL QAGS adaptive integration — integrate_gsl
text: f function: scalar -> scalar whose integral seek numeric: bracket left endpoint b numeric: bracket right endpoint tol numeric: relative tolerance (default 1e-8)

title: GSL QAGS adaptive integration — integrate_gsl
text: Returns numeric scalar representing approximate integral f [, b].

what: Knock-in / knock-out options pricing via Monte Carlo simulation — mc_price_barrier
title: Knock-in / knock-out options pricing via Monte Carlo simulation — mc_price_barrier
text: Computes theoretical price knock-/ knock-option using Monte Carlo Geometric Brownian Motion paths.

title: Knock-in / knock-out options pricing via Monte Carlo simulation — mc_price_barrier
code: mc_price_barrier(   S,   K,   T,   r,   sigma,   N_paths,   N_steps,   B,   is_upper,   is_knockout,   is_call )

title: Knock-in / knock-out options pricing via Monte Carlo simulation — mc_price_barrier
text: S numeric scalar: current price underlying asset K numeric scalar: strike price option T numeric scalar: time expiration option (years) r numeric scalar: risk-free interest rate (annualized) sigma numeric scalar: volatility underlying asset (annualized) N_paths integer scalar: number simulated paths N_steps integer scalar: number time steps per path B numeric scalar: barrier level is_upper integer scalar: 1, active S >= B; 0, active S <= B is_knockout integer scalar: 1, barrier deactivates option; 0, barrier activates option is_call integer scalar: 1, price call option; 0, price put option

title: Knock-in / knock-out options pricing via Monte Carlo simulation — mc_price_barrier
text: Returns numeric scalar range (0, +inf) representing price option.

title: Knock-in / knock-out options pricing via Monte Carlo simulation — mc_price_barrier

Merton jump-diffusion path simulation — merton_paths
text: Simulates asset price paths Merton jump-diffusion model: GBM compound Poisson jumps.

title: Merton jump-diffusion path simulation — merton_paths
code: merton_paths(S0, mu, sigma, lambda, mu_j, sigma_j, T, N_steps, N_paths)

title: Merton jump-diffusion path simulation — merton_paths
text: S0 numeric scalar: initial asset price mu numeric scalar: drift (annualized) sigma numeric scalar: diffusion volatility (annualized) lambda numeric scalar: jump intensity (jumps per year) mu_j numeric scalar: mean log-jump size sigma_j numeric scalar: std log-jump size T numeric scalar: time horizon (years) N_steps integer scalar: number time steps N_paths integer scalar: number simulated paths

title: Merton jump-diffusion path simulation — merton_paths
text: Returns numeric matrix dimensions N_steps x N_paths. column one path.

what: GBM parameter calibration via Metropolis-Hastings MCMC — mh_sampler_gbm
title: GBM parameter calibration via Metropolis-Hastings MCMC — mh_sampler_gbm
text: Calibrates mu sigma Geometric Brownian Motion model log-return series using Metropolis-Hastings algorithm.

title: GBM parameter calibration via Metropolis-Hastings MCMC — mh_sampler_gbm
code: mh_sampler_gbm(   returns,   n_iter,   n_burning,   proposal_mu = 0.005,   proposal_sigma = 0.005,   mu_init = 0,   sigma_init = 0.2,   dt = 1/252 )

title: GBM parameter calibration via Metropolis-Hastings MCMC — mh_sampler_gbm
text: returns numeric vector: log-return series n_iter integer scalar: total MCMC iterations n_burning integer scalar: burn-iterations discard proposal_mu numeric scalar: random walk step size mu proposal_sigma numeric scalar: random walk step size sigma mu_init numeric scalar: initial value mu (default 0) sigma_init numeric scalar: initial value sigma (default 0.2) dt numeric scalar: time step observations (default 1/252)

title: GBM parameter calibration via Metropolis-Hastings MCMC — mh_sampler_gbm
text: Returns numeric matrix dimensions (n_iter - n_burnin) x 2. Columns mu sigma.

title: GBM parameter calibration via Metropolis-Hastings MCMC — mh_sampler_gbm
Bisection root finding — rootfind_bisect
text: Finds root f(x) = 0 [, b] using bisection.

title: Bisection root finding — rootfind_bisect
text: f function: scalar -> scalar whose root seek numeric: bracket left endpoint b numeric: bracket right endpoint tol numeric: convergence tolerance (default 1e-10) max_iter integer: maximum iterations (default 1000)

title: Bisection root finding — rootfind_bisect
text: Returns numeric scalar representing approximate root f.

title: Newton-Raphson root finding — rootfind_newton
text: Finds root f(x) = 0 using Newton-Raphson method.

title: Newton-Raphson root finding — rootfind_newton
text: f function: scalar -> scalar whose root seek df function: derivative f x0 numeric: initial guess root tol numeric: convergence tolerance (default 1e-10) max_iter integer: maximum iterations (default 1000)

title: Newton-Raphson root finding — rootfind_newton
text: Returns numeric scalar representing approximate root f.
